channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump returns to site of attempted assassination, honors shooting victims
item.description: Former President Donald Trump is returning Saturday to the site of his attempted assassination with a rally in Butler County.
item.dc:creator: Patty Coller, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Eric Adams channels Trump as he ramps up revenge accusations against the Biden admin, expert says
item.description: Two New Yorkers on opposite sides of the political spectrum have a similar beef with the federal government — allegations of politically motivated prosecution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Social Security COLA raise: How much will recipients get in 2025?
item.description: The Social Security Administration is less than two weeks away from announcing its cost of living adjustment (COLA), the annual bump in recipients' payments to help them deal with inflation.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KTLA 5

item.title: New judge could give Diddy another attempt to win bail
item.description: While Diddy's legal team hasn’t commented, the assignment of a new judge potentially opens the door for a third bail hearing.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, KTLA 5

item.title: Death toll from Hurricane Helene rises to 227 as grim task of recovering bodies continues
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — The death toll from Hurricane Helene inched up to 227 on Saturday as the grim task of recovering bodies continued more than a week after the monster storm ravaged the Southeast and killed people in six states. Helene came ashore Sept.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dead whale washes ashore in Queens after being seen floating in New York waters
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A dead whale washed ashore at Fort Tilden in Queens on Saturday morning, according to a Facebook post by the Atlantic Marine Conservation Society. The dead whale was first discovered floating in the waters surrounding New York on Thursday. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, NewsNation

item.title: Dog alerts family of fire helping them escape
item.description: Officials with the Spearman Volunteer Fire Department released information regarding a mobile home fire that happened Saturday morning.
item.dc:creator: Dailyn Wells, NewsNation

item.title: Drug lab uncovered after suspicious fire in St. Louis home
item.description: Authorities discovered a drug lab inside a home Friday while investigating a suspicious fire in St. Louis County. The homeowner is now charged and behind bars.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, NewsNation

item.title: County program in Michigan offers savings for veterans
item.description: Kalamazoo County veterans can now get extra savings from area businesses thanks to a new rewards program launched by the Office of County Clerk and Register of Deeds this week.
item.dc:creator: Josh Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Former New York governor and stepson assaulted during evening walk
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former New York Gov. David Paterson and his stepson sustained minor injuries when they were assaulted on a Manhattan street by four men and a woman as they went for an evening walk, police said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former Capitol Police officer recounts Jan. 6: 'It's hard to relive'
item.description: Former Capitol Hill Police Officer Winston Pingeon recalled the events of Jan. 6, 2021, when rioters breached the Capitol in an apparent attempt to stop Congress’s certification of the Electoral College results, in an interview with ABC News. "It's hard to relive and talk about moments like this.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KRON4

item.title: Many loathe public speakerphone calls, so why do people do it?
item.description: Few things are as universally reviled as a loud phone conversation infringing on your personal space. (Mosquitoes and taxes, aside.) Doing it on loudspeaker is even more incendiary.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: After the deluge, the lies: Misinformation and hoaxes about Helene cloud the recovery
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The facts emerging from Hurricane Helene's destruction are heartrending: Businesses and homes destroyed, whole communities nearly wiped out, hundreds of lives lost, hundreds of people missing.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Does a cumulus cloud really weigh a million pounds?
item.description: Just because something floats doesn't mean it is weightless. A great example is when oil is poured onto water. The oil will float on top of the water, but that doesn't mean it has no weight.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Bacca, Fox 5

item.title: These cities have the worst drivers in the U.S., study says
item.description: A Tennessee city has once again been given the dubious title of home to the worst drivers in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Inside the North Carolina mountain town that Hurricane Helene nearly wiped off the map
item.description: Nearly 400 miles from where Hurricane Helene made landfall, the North Carolina mountain hamlet of Chimney Rock Village has been all but wiped off the map.
item.dc:creator: Allen G. Breed, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Supreme Court opens its new term with election disputes in the air but not yet on the docket
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Transgender rights, the regulation of “ghost guns” and the death penalty highlight the Supreme Court's election-season term that begins Monday, with the prospect of the court's intervention in voting disputes lurking in the background.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ohio woman's viral videos about rug buried in her yard led to search for potential body
item.description: An Ohio woman shared videos on her TikTok, concerned that a body may have been buried in her yard, leading to a police search of her property.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former New York Gov. David Paterson, stepson attacked by group of suspects while walking dog
item.description: Former New York Gov. David Paterson and his stepson were attacked by a group of suspects in Manhattan on Friday night when they were walking their dog.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Texas execution is renewing calls for clemency. It's rarely granted
item.description: Texas rarely grants clemency for death row inmates, but that's not stopping a group of bipartisan lawmakers from showing their support for a man scheduled to be executed in October.
item.dc:creator: Nadia Lathan And Summer Ballentine, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Well-known Asheville music tradition returns in a sign of hopefulness after Helene
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (AP) — A well-known Asheville musical tradition returned Friday night, in a sign of hopefulness a week after Helene battered the mountain city. The Asheville Drum Circle had its first regular Friday night session since the powerful storm blew in.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Idaho state senator tells Native American candidate 'go back where you came from' in forum
item.description: KENDRICK, Idaho (AP) — Tensions rose during a bipartisan forum this week after an audience question about discrimination reportedly led an Idaho state senator to angrily tell a Native American candidate to “go back where you came from.” Republican Sen.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bands drop out of Orlando music festival after Kyle Rittenhouse booked as guest
item.description: Shell Shock organizers describe the festival as a concert charity event that seeks to raise funds for a charity that "supports first responders struggling with injuries to include Post Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD).
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, KRON4

item.title: Ex- Virginia cop who killed shoplifting suspect acquitted of manslaughter, guilty on firearm charge
item.description: FAIRFAX, Va. (AP) — A jury on Friday acquitted a former police officer in Virginia of involuntary manslaughter after he fatally shot a shoplifting suspect outside a busy shopping mall.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BARAKAT and OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: When will the power return? Weary Carolinas residents long for relief after Helene's fury
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (AP) — The weary and worn residents of Julianne Johnson's neighborhood in Asheville have been getting by without electricity since Hurricane Helene tore through the Southeast last week and upended their lives.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why are there still so many blackouts after major storms?
item.description: The high costs of infrastructure improvements likely have contributed to the blackouts. Experts say more can be done to prevent power outages, but it would require significant investments.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WGN 9

item.title: McDonald's adding Chicken Big Mac to US menus for a limited time
item.description: McDonald's is set to introduce the Chicken Big Mac, a reimagined version of its classic Big Mac, featuring two tempura-battered chicken patties on a sesame seed bun, on Oct. 10.
item.dc:creator: Nick Gladney, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Del Taco rolls out new value meals starting at $4.49
item.description: Del Taco has introduced a new lineup of value meals, joining a growing number of fast-food chains that are choosing to offer more low-cost food items as inflation continues to impact customers’ wallets.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Obama to campaign for Harris in key swing states, starting with Pennsylvania
item.description: Former President Obama is set to hit the campaign trail for Vice President Harris this month, starting his blitz in the key battleground state of Pennsylvania next week.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Supreme Court declines to block Biden limits on methane, toxic pollution
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday declined to block Biden administration limits on planet-warming methane from oil and gas production and toxic pollution from coal plants.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Helene’s powerful storm surge killed 12 near Tampa. They didn't have to die
item.description: INDIAN ROCKS BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Aiden Bowles was stubborn, so even as Florida officials told residents of the barrier island north of St. Petersburg that Hurricane Helene's storm surge could be deadly, the retired restaurant owner stayed put.
item.dc:creator: DAVID FISCHER and TERRY SPENCER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Most visited national park site closed indefinitely due to Hurricane Helene
item.description: It comes right as the region – and road – typically sees a boom in usage as weather cools and the leaves change colors.
item.dc:creator: Doug Coats, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Country music star Garth Brooks accused of sexual assault by makeup artist
item.description: Garth Brooks, the country music superstar who gained fame in the 1990s with several hit songs, has been named in a lawsuit accusing him of raping a hair and makeup artist in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: VIDEO: Shipping container slams into electrical pole during Hurricane Helene; death toll reaches 200
item.description: A shipping container slammed into an electrical pole while floating debris was split in half during extreme, rising water in Asheville, North Carolina last week.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Liz Cheney to join Harris on campaign trail at birthplace of GOP
item.description: Cheney and her father, former GOP vice president Dick Cheney, endorsed Harris last month over GOP nominee Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: US school-entry vaccination rates fall as exemptions keep rising
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. kindergarten vaccination rates dipped last year and the proportion of children with exemptions rose to an all-time high, according to federal data posted Wednesday. The share of kids exempted from vaccine requirements rose to 3.3%, up from 3% the year before.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Houston teens carjack driver to smuggle migrants
item.description: Two men have pleaded guilty to a carjacking in South Texas where they said they stole a vehicle in order to smuggle migrants into the country.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5
